title: rubus subincisus
intro: Rubus subincisus, also known as the serrated raspberry or mountain raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus subincisus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. Rubus subincisus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.
